 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hzf","title":"Create root layout with providers (app/layout.tsx)","description":"**Details:**
- Root layout wrapping entire application
- Includes: ThemeProvider, ToastProvider, BasketProvider
- Sets up Geist fonts
- Includes metadata for SEO
- Includes SpotlightSearch (global Cmd+K)

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx
import { Geist, Geist_Mono } from 'next/font/google';
import { ThemeProvider } from '@/components/theme-provider';
import { ToastProvider } from '@/components/toast-provider';
import { BasketProvider } from '@/contexts/basket-context';
import { SpotlightSearch } from '@/components/spotlight-search';
import { Nav } from '@/components/nav';
import { Footer } from '@/components/footer';
import './globals.css';

const geistSans = Geist({ subsets: ['latin'], variable: '--font-geist-sans' });
const geistMono = Geist_Mono({ subsets: ['latin'], variable: '--font-geist-mono' });

export const metadata = {
  title: {
    default: 'Jeffrey\'s Prompts',
    template: '%s | Jeffrey\'s Prompts'
  },
  description: 'Curated prompts for agentic coding. Browse, copy, install as Claude Code skills.',
  metadataBase: new URL('https://jeffreysprompts.com'),
  openGraph: {
    title: 'Jeffrey\'s Prompts',
    description: 'Curated prompts for agentic coding',
    url: 'https://jeffreysprompts.com',
    siteName: 'JeffreysPrompts.com',
    type: 'website',
  },
  twitter: {
    card: 'summary_large_image',
    creator: '@doodlestein',
  },
};

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang='en' suppressHydrationWarning>
      <body className={`${geistSans.variable} ${geistMono.variable} font-sans antialiased`}>
        <ThemeProvider>
          <ToastProvider>
            <BasketProvider>
              <Nav />
              <main className='min-h-screen'>{children}</main>
              <Footer />
              <SpotlightSearch />
            </BasketProvider>
          </ToastProvider>
        </ThemeProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

**Provider Order (outer to inner):**
1. ThemeProvider (affects all styling)
2. ToastProvider (toasts can appear anywhere)
3. BasketProvider (used by prompts throughout)

**Location:** apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx

**Reasoning:** Root layout establishes the foundation for the entire app. Providers must wrap in correct order for proper functionality. Global components (Nav, Footer, SpotlightSearch) should be included here.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:36:05.668478385-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:24:25.113425847-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:24:25.113425847-05:00","close_reason":"Completed as part of Next.js app and core package setup. Barrel exports, root layout, main page, and API routes are all functional.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hzf","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:02.063567961-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hzf","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:02.111898234-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hzf","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ixb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:02.138226362-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hzf","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-u36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:02.164282467-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-iczz","title":"E2E Tests: Mobile Responsive Layouts","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-oe5. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Create E2E tests for mobile responsive layouts and touch interactions.

## Test Scenarios

### Viewport Sizes
1. Mobile portrait (375px)
2. Mobile landscape (667px)
3. Tablet portrait (768px)
4. Tablet landscape (1024px)
5. Desktop (1280px+)

### Navigation
6. Hamburger menu on mobile
7. Menu opens/closes correctly
8. Sidebar collapses appropriately
9. Bottom nav (if applicable)
10. Sticky header behavior

### Content Layout
11. Cards stack on mobile
12. Tables scroll horizontally
13. Forms are full-width
14. Images scale correctly
15. Text is readable (16px min)

### Touch Interactions
16. Touch targets 44px minimum
17. Swipe gestures work
18. Long press menus work
19. Pull to refresh (if applicable)
20. No hover-only interactions

### Performance
21. LCP acceptable on 3G
22. No layout shift on load
23. Images lazy load
24. Smooth scrolling

## Files to Create
- e2e/mobile/viewports.spec.ts
- e2e/mobile/navigation.spec.ts
- e2e/mobile/touch.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/mobile-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- All viewports tested
- Touch interactions work
- Performance acceptable on mobile

## Logging Requirements
- Log viewport size for each run
- Record Core Web Vitals snapshots per page
- Capture screenshots for all viewports

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-klux","title":"EPIC: Public Profiles & Reputation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-61i.

## Overview
Public user profiles and author reputation system for the community.

## Public Profile Page (/user/[username])

### Profile Header
- Avatar
- Display name
- Username
- Bio
- Join date
- Social links
- Reputation badges

### Content Tabs
- Public Prompts
- Public Packs
- Public Skills
- Activity (recent public actions)

### Stats Display
- Total public prompts
- Total saves received
- Average rating
- Member since

## Reputation System

### Reputation Score
Calculated from:
- Number of public prompts (+10 each)
- Total saves received (+1 each)
- Positive ratings received (+2 each)
- Age of account
- Featured content (+50 each)

### Badges
- "New Member" - < 30 days
- "Contributor" - 5+ public prompts
- "Popular" - 100+ total saves
- "Top Rated" - Average rating > 90%
- "Featured Author" - Has featured content
- "Founding Member" - Early adopter

### Display
- Badge icons on profile
- Reputation score (optional to show)
- Badge tooltips with criteria

## Database
- Add to user_profiles: username (unique), bio, social_links, is_public
- user_badges (user_id, badge_type, awarded_at)
- Calculate reputation score via materialized view or on-demand

## Privacy
- Users can make profile private (content still public if visibility=public)
- Can hide reputation score
- Can choose which badges to display

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l1t7","title":"Typography Standardization: Eliminate arbitrary text sizes","description":"# Epic: Typography Standardization

## Background & Problem Statement

The UI overhaul established a 5-size typography scale in globals.css:
- text-xs (12px)
- text-sm (14px)
- text-base (16px)
- text-lg (18px)
- text-xl+ (headings)

However, several components still use arbitrary values like \`text-[11px]\` and \`text-[10px]\`, which:
1. Break the design system's rhythm
2. Are harder to maintain
3. Create subtle inconsistencies between similar elements

## Files Affected
1. PromptDetailModal.tsx - 3 instances of text-[11px]
2. SpotlightSearch.tsx - 1 instance of text-[11px], 3 instances of text-[10px]

## The Fix
Replace all arbitrary text sizes with the nearest standard size:
- \`text-[11px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +1px - negligible visual difference)
- \`text-[10px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +2px - slightly larger but more readable)

## Implementation Note
**Typography fixes are included in the color fix tasks:**
- g0oe (PromptDetailModal) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section
- usn5 (SpotlightSearch) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section

This keeps related changes together (same lines of code) and reduces PR churn.

## Design Rationale
- 10-11px text is often too small for comfortable reading
- The extra 1-2px improves accessibility without breaking layouts
- Consistency trumps pixel-perfect sizing for this use case

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero instances of \`text-[Npx]\` in component files
- [ ] All text uses standard Tailwind classes
- [ ] No layout shifts from size changes
- [ ] Text remains readable at all sizes

## Validation
The color validation tests (tzmc) include a typography consistency check that will verify no arbitrary text sizes remain.

## Dependencies
- Typography fixes are bundled into g0oe and usn5 tasks
- tzmc validates the result

## Estimated Scope
- 2 files (via g0oe and usn5)
- 7 class replacements
- Minimal testing required","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:31.250583614-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:27:01.282517492-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l35x","title":"E2E Tests: Admin Dashboard & Moderation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-whb.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the admin dashboard with detailed logging.

## Test Scenarios

### Admin Access Tests
1. **Access Control**
   - Non-admin user cannot access /admin
   - Admin user can access /admin
   - Admin role is verified on each request
   - Tampering with role fails

### User Management Tests
2. **User List**
   - List all users with pagination
   - Search users by email/name
   - Filter by tier (free/premium)
   - Filter by status (active/suspended/deleted)
   - Sort by join date, name, etc.

3. **User Detail**
   - View user profile details
   - View subscription history
   - View content (prompts, packs, skills)
   - View activity log

4. **User Actions**
   - Suspend user account
   - Unsuspend user account
   - Grant premium manually
   - Revoke premium
   - Delete user (soft delete)
   - Impersonate user (admin debug)

### Content Moderation Tests
5. **Reported Content**
   - View reported content queue
   - See report details and reason
   - View the reported content
   - Approve content (dismiss reports)
   - Remove content from Swap Meet
   - Warn content creator
   - Ban repeat offenders

6. **Featured Content**
   - Mark prompt as featured
   - Set "Prompt of the Day"
   - Manage featured carousel
   - Schedule featured content

### Analytics Tests
7. **Dashboard Metrics**
   - Total users (by tier)
   - New signups (daily/weekly/monthly)
   - Active users
   - Subscription revenue (MRR)
   - Churn rate
   - Content metrics (prompts, packs, skills)

8. **Export Data**
   - Export user list to CSV
   - Export revenue reports
   - Export content reports

### System Management Tests
9. **Announcements**
   - Create system announcement
   - Set announcement visibility
   - Schedule announcement
   - Dismiss announcement

10. **Maintenance Mode**
    - Enable maintenance mode
    - Users see maintenance page
    - Admins can still access
    - Disable maintenance mode

## Logging Requirements
- Log all admin actions with admin ID
- Log before/after state for user changes
- Log moderation decisions with reason
- Log impersonation sessions
- Screenshot admin dashboard states
- Log analytics query performance

## Test Infrastructure
- Create test admin user
- Create test regular users to manage
- Seed reported content for moderation
- Clean up test data after suite

## Files to Create
- `e2e/admin/access.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/user-management.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/content-moderation.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/analytics.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/system.spec.ts`
- `e2e/lib/admin-helpers.ts`

## Success Criteria
- Admin access properly restricted
- User management flows work
- Moderation actions apply correctly
- Analytics display accurate data
- Audit trail is complete

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l87c","title":"EPIC: Content Reporting & Moderation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-5ly.

## Overview
Content reporting and moderation workflow for community safety.

## Report Flow (User Side)

### 1. Report Button
- Available on all public content (prompts, packs, skills)
- Icon button (flag) with "Report" tooltip
- Only for logged-in users
- Can't report own content

### 2. Report Dialog
- Reason selection:
  - Spam or misleading
  - Inappropriate content
  - Copyright violation
  - Other (with text field)
- Optional additional details
- Submit button

### 3. Confirmation
- "Report submitted" toast
- "We'll review this within 24 hours"

## Moderation Flow (Admin Side)

### 1. Moderation Queue
- List of pending reports
- Sort by: date, report count, severity
- Filter by: status, type, reason

### 2. Review Interface
- View reported content
- View reporter info and reason
- View report history for this content
- Actions:
  - Dismiss report (no action)
  - Warn author
  - Remove content
  - Ban user

### 3. Actions
- Remove: Set visibility to 'removed', notify author
- Warn: Send warning email, record strike
- Ban: Suspend account, notify user

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const contentReports = pgTable("content_reports", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  reporterId: uuid("reporter_id").notNull(),
  contentType: text("content_type").notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  reason: text("reason").notNull(),
  details: text("details"),
  status: text("status").default("pending"),  // pending, reviewed, actioned, dismissed
  reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"),
  reviewedAt: timestamp("reviewed_at"),
  action: text("action"),  // none, warned, removed, banned
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qbem","title":"Implement ratings API endpoints","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-3w1.

## Task
Implement API endpoints for ratings system.

## Endpoints

### POST /api/ratings
Create or update rating.

Request:
```json
{
  "contentType": "user_prompt",
  "contentId": "uuid",
  "value": 5  // 1 (thumbs down) or 5 (thumbs up)
}
```

Response:
- Updated rating stats for the content
- User's current rating

### DELETE /api/ratings/:contentType/:contentId
Remove user's rating.

### GET /api/ratings/:contentType/:contentId
Get rating stats for content.

Response:
```json
{
  "total": 150,
  "positive": 142,
  "negative": 8,
  "approvalRate": 94.7,
  "userRating": 5  // null if not rated
}
```

### GET /api/ratings/user
Get user's rating history.

Query params:
- contentType: filter by type
- page, limit

## Implementation Notes

### Rating Stats Update
On rating change:
1. Update or insert rating record
2. Recalculate rating_stats for content
3. Return updated stats

Use database transaction to ensure consistency.

### Rate Limiting
- Max 60 ratings per user per hour
- Prevent rating spam

### Edge Cases
- User rates same content twice (update, don't duplicate)
- Content deleted while rating (graceful failure)
- Rating own content (allowed but discouraged in UI)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qg15","title":"Implement bundle size optimization and monitoring","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-lppg. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Optimize JavaScript bundle size and set up monitoring.

## Why This Matters
- Large bundles = slow load
- Mobile users on slow networks
- Core Web Vitals impact
- SEO ranking factor

## Optimization Targets
- Initial bundle < 100KB gzipped
- Per-route chunks < 50KB
- Total JS < 300KB gzipped
- No unused dependencies

## Strategies

### 1. Code Splitting
```typescript
// Dynamic imports for routes
const AdminDashboard = dynamic(() => import('@/app/admin/dashboard'), {
  loading: () => <Skeleton />,
});

// Split heavy components
const Editor = dynamic(() => import('@/components/editor'), {
  ssr: false,
  loading: () => <EditorSkeleton />,
});
```

### 2. Tree Shaking
```typescript
// Import specific functions
import { format } from 'date-fns/format';  // Good
// import { format } from 'date-fns';       // Bad - imports all

// Import specific icons
import { Plus } from 'lucide-react';  // Good
// import * as Icons from 'lucide-react';  // Bad
```

### 3. Dependency Audit
```typescript
// package.json - analyze with bundlephobia
{
  "scripts": {
    "analyze": "ANALYZE=true next build",
    "bundle-size": "size-limit"
  }
}

// .size-limit.json
[
  {
    "path": ".next/static/chunks/*.js",
    "limit": "300 KB"
  }
]
```

### 4. Lazy Loading
```typescript
// Lazy load below-fold components
const Features = lazy(() => import('./features'));
const Testimonials = lazy(() => import('./testimonials'));

// Use Intersection Observer
function LazySection({ children }) {
  const [isVisible, setIsVisible] = useState(false);
  const ref = useRef();
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => {
      if (entry.isIntersecting) setIsVisible(true);
    });
    observer.observe(ref.current);
    return () => observer.disconnect();
  }, []);
  
  return <div ref={ref}>{isVisible ? children : <Placeholder />}</div>;
}
```

## Monitoring

### Bundle Analysis Report
```typescript
// Generate report on build
// .github/workflows/bundle-size.yml
- name: Analyze bundle
  run: |
    npm run build
    npm run analyze -- --json > bundle-stats.json
    
- name: Comment PR with bundle size
  uses: actions/github-script@v6
  with:
    script: |
      const stats = require('./bundle-stats.json');
      // Compare with main branch
      // Comment on PR if increase > 5%
```

### Size Budget CI Check
```yaml
# Fail if bundle too large
- name: Check bundle size
  run: npx size-limit
```

## Optimization Checklist
- [ ] Remove unused dependencies
- [ ] Use dynamic imports for routes
- [ ] Lazy load heavy components
- [ ] Import specific functions only
- [ ] Use next/image for images
- [ ] Enable gzip/brotli compression
- [ ] Preload critical resources
- [ ] Defer non-critical scripts

## Files to Create
- .size-limit.json
- scripts/analyze-bundle.ts
- .github/workflows/bundle-size.yml
- next.config.js (optimization settings)

## Success Criteria
- Initial load < 100KB gzipped
- Lighthouse performance > 90
- No regression in CI
- Bundle report on each PR

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qqlc","title":"Mobile UX Enhancements: Gesture discoverability and touch targets","description":"# Epic: Mobile UX Enhancements

## Background & Problem Statement

The mobile experience includes sophisticated features (swipe gestures, long-press menus) that are completely hidden from users. This is a discoverability problem that reduces feature adoption.

### Issue 1: Swipe Gestures Are Hidden
**Current state:** Users must discover swipe left/right by accident
**Problem:**
- Power features go unused
- Mobile experience feels identical to desktop (just smaller)
- No onboarding or hints

### Issue 2: Touch Targets Too Small
**Current state:** Some interactive elements < 44px
**Locations:**
- SpotlightSearch category pills: ~28px height
- BasketSidebar remove buttons: 28px
- Some badge buttons

### Issue 3: First-Time User Experience
**Current state:** No guidance for mobile-specific features
**Problem:**
- Users don't know about double-tap to favorite
- Long-press for quick actions is undiscovered
- Swipe actions never used

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Swipe hint shown on first card (dismissible)
- [ ] All touch targets ≥ 44px
- [ ] Gesture hints stored in localStorage (show once)
- [ ] Visual edge indicators on cards (optional)
- [ ] Quick actions menu has clear trigger

## Design Principles
- Teach by showing, not telling
- Respect user attention (don't overdo hints)
- Make gestures feel native
- Follow iOS/Android conventions

## Dependencies
- Should be done AFTER color consistency
- Mobile-only changes, low risk to desktop

## Estimated Scope
- 3-4 files to modify
- Some new components (SwipeHint, TouchTarget wrapper)
- Requires localStorage for persistence","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:39:06.621816378-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:39:06.621816378-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qytk","title":"E2E Tests: Moderation & Spam Controls","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-gx6z.

## Task
Create E2E tests for community reporting and spam prevention flows.

## Why This Matters
- Protects community quality
- Prevents abuse and legal risk
- Ensures legitimate users are not blocked

## Test Scenarios

### Spam Detection
1. Submit content with known spam pattern and verify block
2. Submit borderline content and verify soft-block state
3. Verify user-facing reason + appeal path

### Reporting Flow
1. Report content as spam
2. Verify success toast and report stored
3. Prevent duplicate reports from same user

### Moderation Review
1. Admin opens moderation queue
2. Approve/Reject actions update content visibility
3. Verify audit log entry created

## Logging Requirements
- Log spam score + rule hits (redacted)
- Log report creation payload + requestId
- Screenshot report dialog + moderation queue

## Files to Create
- e2e/moderation/spam-detection.spec.ts
- e2e/moderation/reporting.spec.ts
- e2e/moderation/review.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/moderation-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Spam rules enforced without false positives
- Reporting works end-to-end
- Moderation actions persist correctly
- Logs support triage and tuning

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-r4c7","title":"Implement DMCA and copyright handling process","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-gmni.

## Task
Create process for handling DMCA takedown requests and copyright claims.

## Why This Matters
- Legal compliance (DMCA Safe Harbor)
- Protect platform from liability
- Support content creators
- Handle disputes fairly

## Process

### 1. DMCA Takedown Request
- Public submission form
- Required information (claimant, content, signature)
- Notification to content owner
- 48-hour review period

### 2. Counter-Notice
- Allow content owner to dispute
- Required counter-notice format
- 10-14 day waiting period
- Restore if no lawsuit filed

### 3. Repeat Infringers
- Track strike count
- 3 strikes policy
- Account termination
- Appeals process

## Submission Form Fields
```typescript
interface DMCARequest {
  // Claimant info
  claimantName: string;
  claimantEmail: string;
  claimantAddress: string;
  
  // Content info
  copyrightedWorkDescription: string;
  copyrightedWorkUrl?: string;
  infringingContentUrl: string;
  
  // Legal attestations
  goodFaithStatement: boolean;
  accuracyStatement: boolean;
  ownershipStatement: boolean;
  
  // Signature
  signature: string;
  signatureDate: Date;
}
```

## Database
```typescript
export const dmcaRequests = pgTable('dmca_requests', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // Claimant
  claimantName: text('claimant_name').notNull(),
  claimantEmail: text('claimant_email').notNull(),
  
  // Content
  contentType: text('content_type').notNull(),
  contentId: uuid('content_id').notNull(),
  contentOwnerId: uuid('content_owner_id').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  
  // Status
  status: text('status').default('pending'), // pending, removed, restored, dismissed
  
  // Counter-notice
  counterNoticeAt: timestamp('counter_notice_at'),
  counterNoticeContent: text('counter_notice_content'),
  
  // Resolution
  resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at'),
  resolvedBy: uuid('resolved_by'),
  resolution: text('resolution'),
  
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const copyrightStrikes = pgTable('copyright_strikes', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  dmcaRequestId: uuid('dmca_request_id').references(() => dmcaRequests.id),
  reason: text('reason').notNull(),
  issuedAt: timestamp('issued_at').defaultNow(),
  expiresAt: timestamp('expires_at'), // Strikes may expire after time
});
```

## Workflow
1. Receive DMCA request
2. Validate required fields
3. Take down content immediately
4. Notify content owner
5. Owner can submit counter-notice
6. Wait 10-14 days
7. Restore or keep down based on outcome

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/dmca/page.tsx (form)
- src/app/api/dmca/route.ts
- src/lib/legal/dmca.ts
- src/app/(app)/admin/dmca/page.tsx

## Success Criteria
- DMCA form accessible
- Takedowns processed promptly
- Counter-notice process works
- Strike system enforced
